title: Vishnu Sahasranama
intro: A sacred composition of 1000 names of Lord Vishnu, the preserver and sustainer of the universe, recited for protection and spiritual elevation.
description:
The Vishnu Sahasranama (also known as Vishnusahasranama Stotra) is one of the most important and sacred texts in Hinduism. It is a collection of 1000 names of Lord Vishnu, each representing a different attribute or quality of the supreme deity.

|| Opening ||
Vishnur Mukhyapranava Stotram, Mahabhagavata Vishnoh.
Sahasra Nama Stotram, Jnana Yoga Samagamam.

It is traditionally recited during Auspicious occasions and for spiritual growth.
meaning: The Vishnu Sahasranama celebrates the infinite qualities and forms of Lord Vishnu. Each name reveals a different aspect of divine consciousness and cosmic order.

faq.a1: Sahasranama means '1000 names'. This stotra contains 1000 divine names of Lord Vishnu.
faq.q2: How long does it take to recite?
faq.a2: Approximately 45 minutes to 1 hour depending on the pace of recitation.
faq.a3: Divine protection, freedom from obstacles, spiritual wisdom, and moksha (liberation).
